Discover
The Gonzalez Family Legacy

90+ years of farming
the same volcanic land.

The Gonzalez family has farmed coffee in Boquete, Panama for over 90 years. What was once our grandfather's land now lives on through our family, our uncle's farm, and trusted friends who became stewards of the same soil.

Every bean we source comes from that unbroken legacy — whoever tends the land today, the roots go back to the same volcanic highlands, the same family, the same story.

Read the full story →
90+
Years of Gonzalez Family
Coffee Farming in Boquete

"What arrives in your cup has a name, a place, and a lineage that goes back generations."

— Alex Evans, Founder · Café Boquete Coffee Co

Pictured: waterfalls on land once owned by the Gonzalez family, Boquete, Panamá

The Green Bean

Finca Gonzalez · Boquete

The Tradition

Handcrafted · Generation to Generation

From our family's
land to your cup.

Every step of our process is rooted in the traditions the Gonzalez family has practiced for over 90 years — from hand-sorting cherries at harvest to natural sun-drying on the volcanic highlands of Boquete.

We roast light to medium to honor what the land already gives us. The flavor is in the bean. Our job is to get out of its way.

Coffee Cherry Tea

Cascara
by the pound

Gonzalez Family · Finca Gonzalez · Boquete, Panamá

Long before cascara had a name in specialty coffee, our family was brewing it. The sun-dried skin of the coffee cherry — naturally sweet, fruity, earthy. A taste of the whole plant, not just the bean.

How to Brew

Hot: 2 tbsp per 8oz at 190°F, steep 4 minutes.
Cold: 3 tbsp per 12oz cold water, steep overnight, strain and serve over ice.
